Planning the design system architecture. Tokens and theme classes will be created first. Then layout, navigation, data, form, and feedback components will follow.

Adding dialog components to the design system plan. AppFilterPanel is being skipped for now because the phase document excludes it from the first wave.

AppStatusChip will support both string and enum status values. AppDataTable will use MudTable rather than MudDataGrid. Token contrast tests will be plain xUnit tests in Domain.Tests or Integration.Tests.

Token contrast tests will go in Integration.Tests because they reference Web project tokens. The bUnit package needs to be added to Integration.Tests and Directory.Packages.props. The dialog service will use IAppDialogService with ConfirmAsync, backed by AppDialogService using IDialogService, plus the AppConfirmDialog component.

AppStatusChip will map each enum value to a specific status type. MainLayout will receive a full rebrand with a skip link, AppTopNavigation, AppSideNavigation, AppFooter, and removal of the inline theme.

A dev-only DesignSystemShowcase page at /design-system will be gated behind environment checks. MainLayout.razor.css may be removed since error UI styles now live in app.css. AppPage will use MudBlazor's BreadcrumbItem. AppDataTable will be a generic component with a cascading type parameter.

IAppDialogService will be registered in Program.cs. The Open Sans WOFF2 font files will be downloaded directly from Google Fonts via curl into the wwwroot/fonts directory.

The font download strategy is refined to use Google Fonts CDN URLs matching the spec filenames exactly. Implementation will proceed systematically across all planned files. AppStatusChip will be updated to accept the RegistrationCaseStatus enum directly, avoiding domain changes as required by the phase constraints.

AppStatusChip will accept a string label and attempt to parse it into the enum internally. This avoids changing domain models while still supporting enum values. The list page handler returns a string status, so the summary DTO may be updated to an enum at the web layer without touching domain code.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Implementing Phase 3 systematically: creating the design system foundation, then components, layout, page retrofits, and tests.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Adding the missing `PageTitle` import to `DesignSystem/_Imports.razor`.

Fixing AppCard header ordering for MudBlazor 8. Removing conditional PagerContent in AppDataTable. Adding routing and forms namespaces to imports. Updating DesignSystemShowcase with required usings and MudTextField type parameter.

AppCard will conditionally render the avatar and actions while keeping the content always visible. AppDataTable will either always render the pager or bind AllowPaging directly to MudTablePager, since MudBlazor rejects an `@if` wrapper around PagerContent.

The simplest fix for AppDataTable is to always include PagerContent and disable the MudTablePager when paging is off. Splitting the component into two table variants would be too heavy.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Fixing compile errors.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
AppCard cannot use conditional rendering inside MudCardHeader. All sections must be rendered unconditionally or handled separately.

AppDataTable will drop its conditional ColGroup. AppPage needs a PageTitle using directive. AppSearchBar requires EventCallback.Factory.Create for ValueChanged. RegistrationCaseList should remove OnClick:PreventDefault from MudLink.

AppCard will use a MudStack row layout for the header, conditionally rendering the icon and title together. Header actions will follow conditionally.

Conditional blocks inside MudCardHeader are invalid in MudBlazor 8. AppCard will move the title into MudCardContent instead.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Phase 3 is implemented. Here's what landed:

## Design system foundation

**`DesignSystem/`** in the Web project with:
- **`SchaerbeekTheme`** + token classes (`SchaerbeekColors`, `SchaerbeekTypography`, `SchaerbeekSpacing`, etc.)
- **Self-hosted Open Sans** (400/600/700) in `wwwroot/fonts/`
- **`app.css` rewrite** — focus ring, skip link, validation colors, print rules, footer styling

## UI kit (`App*` wrappers)

| Category | Components |
|----------|------------|
| Layout | `AppPage`, `AppPageHeader`, `AppSection`, `AppCard`, `AppToolbar`, `AppActionBar` |
| Navigation | `AppTopNavigation`, `AppSideNavigation`, `AppBreadcrumb`, `AppFooter` |
| Data | `AppDataTable`, `AppStatusChip`, `AppSearchBar` |
| Forms | `AppFormSection`, `AppFieldLabel`, `AppSaveCancelBar` |
| Feedback | `AppAlert`, `AppEmptyState`, `AppLoading`, `AppInfoBox` |
| Dialogs | `AppConfirmDialog`, `AppDialogOptions`, `IAppDialogService` |

## Layout rebrand

`MainLayout` now uses the Schaerbeek theme, skip link, branded app bar/drawer/footer, and a dev-only **Design system** nav item.

## Phase 1 retrofit

- **`/registration/cases`** — `AppPage` + `AppDataTable` + `AppStatusChip` + `AppEmptyState` + search
- **Case detail** — `AppFormSection`, `AppSaveCancelBar`, `AppInfoBox`, status chips
- **Home**, **NewCaseDialog**, **DocumentUpload** — aligned to the kit

## Style guide

**`/design-system`** (Development only) — token swatches, typography, and wrapper examples.

## Tests

- **bUnit** component tests for core wrappers
- **WCAG contrast tests** for palette pairs
- All **32 integration + 4 domain tests** pass

---

**Try it:** run AppHost, then visit `/registration/cases` and `/design-system`. You should see Schaerbeek blue (#00537F), Open Sans, turquoise focus rings, and the retrofitted case workflows.

Deferred for later phases (as spec'd): `AppWizard`, `AppTimeline`, `AppFilterPanel`, dark-mode toggle.

---

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The page title is rendering beneath the app header because `AppTopNavigation` places its `MudAppBar` inside `MudLayout` alongside `MudDrawer` and `MudMainContent`.

MudLayout automatically adds top padding to `MudMainContent` when `MudAppBar` is a direct child. `AppTopNavigation` contains no extra wrapper elements, so the markup should be valid.

`FocusOnNavigate` in `Routes.razor` targets `h1` elements. This may scroll the title beneath the fixed app bar during navigation. `MudMainContent` uses `pa-0`, which could override the automatic top padding that `MudLayout` normally applies to main content.

`pa-0` on `MudMainContent` zeroes out all padding, including the top offset that `MudLayout` normally applies to the fixed app bar. The fix is to use `px-0` or `pb-0` instead, or remove `pa-0` entirely and let `AppPage` manage page padding.

`pa-0` likely overrides the default top padding on `.mud-main-content` that MudBlazor applies for the app bar offset. Adding `scroll-margin-top` to the heading or main container would prevent the title from sliding under the fixed header during focus-on-navigate.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

`MudMainContent` has `pa-0`, which likely zeroes the top padding MudBlazor uses to clear the fixed app bar. Checking and fixing that.

Two things were causing this:

1. **`MudMainContent Class="pa-0"`** — MudBlazor uses top padding on main content to clear the fixed app bar (64px). `pa-0` zeroed that out, so page content started at the top of the viewport under the header.

2. **`FocusOnNavigate` targeting `h1`** — on route changes, the browser scrolls the page title into view without accounting for the fixed app bar.

**Changes:**
- Removed `pa-0` from `MudMainContent` in `MainLayout.razor`
- Added `scroll-margin-top: 64px` on `main#main` and `h1` in `app.css` so focused/scrolled titles sit below the app bar

Refresh the app — the page title should now appear fully below the header.

The layout likely isn't reaching the bottom because MudLayout uses height: 100%, which requires the parent container to have a defined height. Blazor typically needs html and body elements set to height: 100%.

A comprehensive CSS fix is being drafted to force full viewport height and enable vertical stretching. However, MudLayout may not use flexbox for vertical stretching since the main content is a sibling element. A simpler approach is being considered instead.

The simpler fix targets `.app-main-content.mud-main-content` directly. Setting `min-height: 100dvh` and `box-sizing: border-box` forces the element to span the full viewport height. This accounts for the 64px top padding automatically.

The flex children will share the remaining space inside the 100dvh box, pushing the footer to the bottom. MudLayout itself also needs `min-height: 100dvh`, along with the html and body elements. The footer appears to sit below the main content column rather than spanning the full drawer width.

The footer sits above a white gap, confirming it does not reach the viewport bottom. The planned CSS now sets `min-height: 100dvh` on the root elements and MudLayout, then applies `flex: 1 0 auto` to the main content body so it expands properly. The footer will receive `margin-top: auto` as a final safeguard.

_**Agent (default)**_

The gap came from subtracting the app bar height twice: `MudMainContent` already has `padding-top: 64px` for the fixed header, so `min-height: calc(100dvh - 64px)` left the main area ~64px short.
